title: [SimplifyCFG] Optimize select over pointers to eliminate no-op load/store
author: dnsampaio
description:
Transform a pattern where one case of a select over pointers leads to a store(load) of the same address, by avoiding the load and store all together.

Pattern:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %val = load ... %sel
  store ... %val, %a

When %sel chooses %a, the load and store
access the same address - this is a no-op.

This transforms it to:
  br i1 %cond, label %cont, label %do_ldst
do_ldst:
  %val = load ... %b
  store ... %val, %a
  br label %cont
cont:
  ...

tests.test_body:
define void @volatile_store(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond, i64 %idx) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr float, ptr %sel, i64 %idx
  %val = load float, ptr %gep, align 4
  %store_gep = getelementptr float, ptr %a, i64 %idx
  store volatile float %val, ptr %store_gep, align 4
  ret void
}

define void @double_type_aligned(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond, i64 %idx) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr double, ptr %sel, i64 %idx
  %val = load double, ptr %gep, align 8
  %store_gep = getelementptr double, ptr %a, i64 %idx
  store double %val, ptr %store_gep, align 8
  ret void
}

define void @store_different_value(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond, i64 %idx, float %other) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr float, ptr %sel, i64 %idx
  %val = load float, ptr %gep, align 4
  %store_gep = getelementptr float, ptr %a, i64 %idx
  store float %other, ptr %store_gep, align 4
  ret void
}

define float @load_multi_use(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond, i64 %idx) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr float, ptr %sel, i64 %idx
  %val = load float, ptr %gep, align 4
  %store_gep = getelementptr float, ptr %a, i64 %idx
  store float %val, ptr %store_gep, align 4
  ret float %val
}

define void @noop_on_true(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond, i64 %idx) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr float, ptr %sel, i64 %idx
  %val = load float, ptr %gep, align 4
  %store_gep = getelementptr float, ptr %a, i64 %idx
  store float %val, ptr %store_gep, align 4
  ret void
}

define ptr @select_multi_use(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond, i64 %idx) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr float, ptr %sel, i64 %idx
  %val = load float, ptr %gep, align 4
  %store_gep = getelementptr float, ptr %a, i64 %idx
  store float %val, ptr %store_gep, align 4
  ret ptr %sel
}

define void @integer_type(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond, i64 %idx) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr i32, ptr %sel, i64 %idx
  %val = load i32, ptr %gep, align 4
  %store_gep = getelementptr i32, ptr %a, i64 %idx
  store i32 %val, ptr %store_gep, align 4
  ret void
}

define void @direct_ptr_no_gep(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %val = load float, ptr %sel, align 4
  store float %val, ptr %a, align 4
  ret void
}

define void @with_inbounds(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond, i64 %idx) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds float, ptr %sel, i64 %idx
  %val = load float, ptr %gep, align 4
  %store_gep = getelementptr inbounds float, ptr %a, i64 %idx
  store float %val, ptr %store_gep, align 4
  ret void
}

define void @noop_on_false(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond, i64 %idx) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr float, ptr %sel, i64 %idx
  %val = load float, ptr %gep, align 4
  %store_gep = getelementptr float, ptr %b, i64 %idx
  store float %val, ptr %store_gep, align 4
  ret void
}

define void @volatile_load(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond, i64 %idx) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr float, ptr %sel, i64 %idx
  %val = load volatile float, ptr %gep, align 4
  %store_gep = getelementptr float, ptr %a, i64 %idx
  store float %val, ptr %store_gep, align 4
  ret void
}

define ptr @load_gep_multi_use(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond, i64 %idx) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr float, ptr %sel, i64 %idx
  %val = load float, ptr %gep, align 4
  %store_gep = getelementptr float, ptr %a, i64 %idx
  store float %val, ptr %store_gep, align 4
  ret ptr %gep
}

define void @multi_index_gep(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond, i64 %idx1, i64 %idx2) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr [10 x float], ptr %sel, i64 %idx1, i64 %idx2
  %val = load float, ptr %gep, align 4
  %store_gep = getelementptr [10 x float], ptr %a, i64 %idx1, i64 %idx2
  store float %val, ptr %store_gep, align 4
  ret void
}

define void @no_match_store_base(ptr %a, ptr %b, ptr %c, i1 %cond, i64 %idx) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr float, ptr %sel, i64 %idx
  %val = load float, ptr %gep, align 4
  %store_gep = getelementptr float, ptr %c, i64 %idx
  store float %val, ptr %store_gep, align 4
  ret void
}

define void @different_indices(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond, i64 %idx1, i64 %idx2) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr float, ptr %sel, i64 %idx1
  %val = load float, ptr %gep, align 4
  %store_gep = getelementptr float, ptr %a, i64 %idx2
  store float %val, ptr %store_gep, align 4
  ret void
}

define ptr @store_gep_multi_use(ptr %a, ptr %b, i1 %cond, i64 %idx) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, ptr %a, ptr %b
  %gep = getelementptr float, ptr %sel, i64 %idx
  %val = load float, ptr %gep, align 4
  %store_gep = getelementptr float, ptr %a, i64 %idx
  store float %val, ptr %store_gep, align 4
  ret ptr %store_gep
}

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: It's not at all clear to me that this is a profitable transform, at least without profiling information that shows that the branch is well-predicated to be false. This transform is kind of the opposite of what isSafeToSpeculateStore does.

comments.author: dnsampaio
comments.body:
> It's not at all clear to me that this is a profitable transform, at least without profiling information that shows that the branch is well-predicated to be false. This transform is kind of the opposite of what isSafeToSpeculateStore does.

Hi @nikic, thanks for the review.
Indeed we are creating a branch, where simplifycfg generates a select to exactly remove some branching. However, this is a case where the vectorizer can't handle. The idea is just to convert the ir from bad to good from this [example](https://godbolt.org/#g:!((g:!((g:!((h:codeEditor,i:(filename:'1',fontScale:14,fontUsePx:'0',j:1,lang:___c,selection:(endColumn:1,endLineNumber:14,positionColumn:1,positionLineNumber:14,selectionStartColumn:1,selectionStartLineNumber:14,startColumn:1,startLineNumber:14),source:'__attribute__((noinline))%0Avoid+bad(float*+restrict+a,+float*+restrict+b,+float*+restrict+c,+int*+d,+int+N)%7B%0A++++for+(int+i+%3D+0%3B+i+%3C+N%3B+i%2B%2B)%0A++++++++b%5Bi%5D+%3D+d%5Bi%5D+%3F+b%5Bi%5D+:+c%5Bi%5D%3B%0A%7D%0A%0A%0A__attribute__((noinline))%0Avoid+good(float*+restrict+a,+float*+restrict+b,+float*+restrict+c,+int*+d,+int+N)%7B%0A++++for+(int+i+%3D+0%3B+i+%3C+N%3B+i%2B%2B)%0A++++++if+(!!d%5Bi%5D)%0A++++++++b%5Bi%5D+%3D+c%5Bi%5D%3B%0A%7D%0A'),l:'5',n:'0',o:'C+source+%231',t:'0')),k:32.10013523117332,l:'4',n:'0',o:'',s:0,t:'0'),(g:!((h:compiler,i:(compiler:rv64-cclang,filters:(b:'0',binary:'1',binaryObject:'1',commentOnly:'0',debugCalls:'1',demangle:'0',directives:'0',execute:'0',intel:'0',libraryCode:'0',trim:'1',verboseDemangling:'0'),flagsViewOpen:'1',fontScale:14,fontUsePx:'0',j:1,lang:___c,libs:!(),options:'-march%3Drv64gcv+-O3',overrides:!(),selection:(endColumn:1,endLineNumber:1,positionColumn:1,positionLineNumber:1,selectionStartColumn:1,selectionStartLineNumber:1,startColumn:1,startLineNumber:1),source:1,wantOptInfo:'0'),l:'5',n:'0',o:'+RISC-V+rv64gc+clang+(trunk)+(Editor+%231)',t:'0')),k:26.428444323574595,l:'4',n:'0',o:'',s:0,t:'0'),(g:!((h:optPipelineView,i:('-fno-discard-value-names':'0',compilerName:'RISC-V+rv64gc+clang+(trunk)',demangle-symbols:'0',dump-full-module:'1',editorid:1,filter-debug-info:'0',filter-inconsequential-passes:'1',filter-instruction-metadata:'0',fontScale:14,fontUsePx:'0',j:1,selectedGroup:bad,selectedIndex:98,sidebarWidth:250,treeid:0),l:'5',n:'0',o:'Opt+Pipeline+Viewer+RISC-V+rv64gc+clang+(trunk)+(Editor+%231,+Compiler+%231)',t:'0')),k:41.47142044525209,l:'4',n:'0',o:'',s:0,t:'0')),l:'2',n:'0',o:'',t:'0')),version:4).

Thinking twice, the important is the case of loops, so I should enforce only the case with GEP.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: If the motivation is vectorization-specific then doing it as a VPlan transform may make more sense. Maybe @fhahn has thoughts on that.
